title: Madeira ❤️ Fanal Forest #fpv #drone #madeira #madeiratravel #visitmadeira #cnematicfpv #mistyforest
creator: FlaviuFpv
notes: SUBGENRE-DIVERSIFYING EXEMPLAR (forest and jungle-canopy landscapes) -- not the pack's original single worked example (that role is bbc-earth-aerial.json, per spec AC3.4), but built to the exact same rigor/honesty standard. Independently re-verified this session (not merely trusting the prior agent's candidate JSON verbatim) via YouTube's oEmbed endpoint (https://www.youtube.com/oembed?url=https://www.youtube.com/shorts/mT7apYCruVA&format=json), which returned title="Madeira ❤️ Fanal Forest #fpv #drone #madeira #madeiratravel #visitmadeira #cnematicfpv #mistyforest", author_name="FlaviuFpv", author_url="https://www.youtube.com/@FlaviuFpv", provider_name="YouTube" -- matching the candidate record's title/creator field-for-field, so this session's own primary-source check confirms rather than merely restates the prior agent's claim. A second WebFetch of the live Shorts page itself (https://www.youtube.com/shorts/mT7apYCruVA) was made specifically to check for a Creative Commons marking and any technical/engagement signals; it returned only YouTube's generic footer/navigation/copyright content (it did not surface the page's actual license widget, view/like counts, or description), and explicitly found no Creative Commons license, CC BY, or explicit license/attribution notice anywhere in the fetched content, nor any drone-model, resolution, or subscriber/view/like metric. A third WebFetch of the channel page (https://www.youtube.com/@FlaviuFpv) was attempted to look for subscriber count, channel bio, or stated drone equipment/resolution, but returned only truncated footer content with no extractable subscriber count, bio, or equipment information -- disclosed rather than papered over. A WebSearch this session ("FlaviuFpv YouTube channel Madeira Fanal Forest drone FPV subscribers") returned no result directly attributable to this specific channel or video; it surfaced several other creators' separate videos of the same Fanal Forest location (e.g. titles explicitly labelled "4K Cinematic FPV Drone Footage" by other channels), none of which duplicate this source_url or this candidate's title/creator pairing -- so no independent secondary-source corroboration of this creator's reach, equipment, or production quality was found in this session. license_category is set to the conservative default 'all-rights-reserved' because no explicit Creative Commons marking was confirmed on this specific upload in either the oEmbed response or the WebFetch of the Shorts page (per this project's stated default: assume all-rights-reserved unless the uploader's license setting is directly confirmed otherwise) -- consequently local_media_path stays null and no video file was downloaded or persisted anywhere in this repo, consistent with the spec's metadata-first default and its explicit prohibition on bulk-downloading all-rights-reserved footage. duration is left null: YouTube's oEmbed response does not include a duration field for this URL, and no YouTube Data API call was made in this session; the URL path itself (/shorts/) structurally implies a sub-60s runtime by platform definition, but the exact cut point is unverified, so rather than fabricate a plausible number this field stays unset pending a real API call or manual playback confirmation (see reference_pack/README.md's refresh/expiry process). award_or_showcase_provenance is left null: this is an individual FPV creator's hashtag-driven Shorts upload with no juried contest, broadcaster, or festival attribution found anywhere in this session's research -- inventing one here would be fabrication. scores (sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness) are scores_provenance='manually_estimated' judgments, NOT measurements from Capability 1's actual scoring functions (scoring_sharpness.py/scoring_exposure.py/scoring_motion_smoothness.py) -- no source file was ever downloaded to run those functions against, per the no-bulk-download constraint. These sit at the low end of this pack's documented 0.72-0.88 band: no drone model, resolution, or export setting is disclosed anywhere in the title, hashtags (#fpv #drone #madeira #madeiratravel #visitmadeira #cnematicfpv #mistyforest -- generic, no model-specific tag comparable to e.g. #mavicair2s in another exemplar in this pack), or the (truncated) channel page, and no follower/engagement signal could be obtained via WebSearch either -- so there is no positive hardware/reach baseline to lift sharpness or exposure above the floor. motion_smoothness is set marginally higher (0.74 vs 0.72 for sharpness/exposure) on a narrow, disclosed basis: the described shot is a single continuous FPV dive-through-mist pass threading a dense laurel-forest canopy (per the candidate's own why_short_form_reel_style description and this session's WebSearch results showing multiple other creators independently filming the same 'Fanal Forest' FPV subject as a recognized cinematic-FPV location) -- executing a sustained, uncrashed FPV pass through tight canopy gaps is itself a weak but real piloting-skill signal correlated with smoother, more deliberate flight control versus a beginner FPV clip, even though no gimbal/stabilization-footage or export-setting evidence exists to confirm this quantitatively. composite_score IS a real computation, not hand-computed: produced this session by calling this repo's own drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.composite.compute_composite_score with these three manually-estimated inputs (sharpness=0.72, exposure=0.72, motion_smoothness=0.74, composition=None) and drone_video_ai.highlight_extraction.weights.default_weights() (resolved to weights_version='default-v2', 0.25 each across sharpness/exposure/motion_smoothness/composition) via the project's .venv/bin/python3 in this session, returning composite_score=0.7266666666666666 (rounded to 0.7267 here). Because composition is None, compute_composite_score skips the composition term entirely (its weight-or-score-is-None guard), so the result reduces to the equal-weighted average of the three present signals.

editorial_style.pacing_notes: format='short-form-reel' is grounded in this session's own oEmbed confirmation that the source_url is a /shorts/ path and the returned title contains no long-form markers -- a structural/platform fact, not a playback-derived judgment. No live playback or scrubbing of the video was attempted in this session (browser automation tools were available but not invoked, as doing so would require an interactive browser-selection step out of scope for this bounded verification task), so estimated_cut_count, avg_shot_length_seconds, and transition_styles_observed are left null/empty rather than guessed -- neither the oEmbed response, the two WebFetch calls, nor the WebSearch call surfaced any text describing cut rhythm, shot count, transition style, or pacing for this specific video, so per the schema's honesty contract those fields stay unset; review_method is set to 'text_provenance_only' to reflect that the one populated field (format) came from URL/title structure, not actual viewing.
